verses.latin_vulgate: Luxuriosa res vinum, et tumultuosa ebrietas :  quicumque his delectatur non erit sapiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ץ הַ֭יַּיִן הֹמֶ֣ה שֵׁכָ֑ר וְכׇל־שֹׁ֥גֶה בּ֝֗וֹ לֹ֣א יֶחְכָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut rugitus leonis, ita et terror regis :  qui provocat eum peccat in animam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֣הַם כַּ֭כְּפִיר אֵ֣ימַת מֶ֑לֶךְ מִ֝תְעַבְּר֗וֹ חוֹטֵ֥א נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The fear of a king is as the roaring of a lion: whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Honor est homini qui separat se a contentionibus ;  omnes autem stulti miscentur contumeliis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כָּב֣וֹד לָ֭אִישׁ שֶׁ֣בֶת מֵרִ֑יב וְכׇל־אֱ֝וִ֗יל יִתְגַּלָּֽע׃
verses.king_james: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter frigus piger arare noluit ;  mendicabit ergo æstate, et non dabitur illi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵ֭חֹרֶף עָצֵ֣ל לֹא־יַחֲרֹ֑שׁ (ישאל) [וְשָׁאַ֖ל] בַּקָּצִ֣יר וָאָֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut aqua profunda, sic consilium in corde viri ;  sed homo sapiens exhauriet illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֣יִם עֲ֭מֻקִּים עֵצָ֣ה בְלֶב־אִ֑ישׁ וְאִ֖ישׁ תְּבוּנָ֣ה יִדְלֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi homines misericordes vocantur ;  virum autem fidelem quis inveniet ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רׇב־אָדָ֗ם יִ֭קְרָא אִ֣ישׁ חַסְדּ֑וֹ וְאִ֥ישׁ אֱ֝מוּנִ֗ים מִ֣י יִמְצָֽא׃
verses.king_james: Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness: but a faithful man who can find?

verses.latin_vulgate: Justus qui ambulat in simplicitate sua  beatos post se filios derelinquet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִתְהַלֵּ֣ךְ בְּתֻמּ֣וֹ צַדִּ֑יק אַשְׁרֵ֖י בָנָ֣יו אַחֲרָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex qui sedet in solio judicii  dissipat omne malum intuitu suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֶ֗לֶךְ יוֹשֵׁ֥ב עַל־כִּסֵּא־דִ֑ין מְזָרֶ֖ה בְעֵינָ֣יו כׇּל־רָֽע׃
verses.king_james: A king that sitteth in the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis potest dicere : Mundum est cor meum ;  purus sum a peccato ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־יֹ֭אמַר זִכִּ֣יתִי לִבִּ֑י טָ֝הַ֗רְתִּי מֵחַטָּאתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?

verses.latin_vulgate: Pondus et pondus, mensura et mensura :  utrumque abominabile est apud Deum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֣בֶן וָ֭אֶבֶן אֵיפָ֣ה וְאֵיפָ֑ה תּוֹעֲבַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה גַּם־שְׁנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Divers weights, and divers measures, both of them are alike abomination to the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex studiis suis intelligitur puer,  si munda et recta sint opera ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֣ם בְּ֭מַעֲלָלָיו יִתְנַכֶּר־נָ֑עַר אִם־זַ֖ךְ וְאִם־יָשָׁ֣ר פׇּעֳלֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work be pure, and whether it be right.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aurem audientem, et oculum videntem :  Dominus fecit utrumque.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹ֣זֶן שֹׁ֭מַעַת וְעַ֣יִן רֹאָ֑ה יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה עָשָׂ֥ה גַם־שְׁנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the Lord hath made even both of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli diligere somnum, ne te egestas opprimat :  aperi oculos tuos, et saturare panibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תֶּאֱהַ֣ב שֵׁ֭נָה פֶּן־תִּוָּרֵ֑שׁ פְּקַ֖ח עֵינֶ֣יךָ שְֽׂבַֽע־לָֽחֶם׃
verses.king_james: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Malum est, malum est, dicit omnis emptor ;  et cum recesserit, tunc gloriabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֣ע רַ֭ע יֹאמַ֣ר הַקּוֹנֶ֑ה וְאֹזֵ֥ל ל֝֗וֹ אָ֣ז יִתְהַלָּֽל׃
verses.king_james: It is naught, it is naught, saith the buyer: but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est aurum et multitudo gemmarum,  et vas pretiosum labia scientiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֣שׁ זָ֭הָב וְרׇב־פְּנִינִ֑ים וּכְלִ֥י יְ֝קָ֗ר שִׂפְתֵי־דָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle vestimentum ejus qui fidejussor extitit alieni,  et pro extraneis aufer pignus ab eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְֽקַח־בִּ֭גְדוֹ כִּי־עָ֣רַב זָ֑ר וּבְעַ֖ד (נכרים) [נׇכְרִיָּ֣ה] חַבְלֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Suavis est homini panis mendacii,  et postea implebitur os ejus calculo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָרֵ֣ב לָ֭אִישׁ לֶ֣חֶם שָׁ֑קֶר וְ֝אַחַ֗ר יִמָּֽלֵא־פִ֥יהוּ חָצָֽץ׃
verses.king_james: Bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cogitationes consiliis roborantur,  et gubernaculis tractanda sunt bella.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֭חֲשָׁבוֹת בְּעֵצָ֣ה תִכּ֑וֹן וּ֝בְתַחְבֻּל֗וֹת עֲשֵׂ֣ה מִלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Every purpose is established by counsel: and with good advice make war.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ei qui revelat mysteria, et ambulat fraudulenter,  et dilatat labia sua, ne commiscearis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גּֽוֹלֶה־סּ֭וֹד הוֹלֵ֣ךְ רָכִ֑יל וּלְפֹתֶ֥ה שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִתְעָרָֽב׃
verses.king_james: He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui maledicit patri suo et matri,  extinguetur lucerna ejus in mediis tenebris :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְ֭קַלֵּל אָבִ֣יו וְאִמּ֑וֹ יִֽדְעַ֥ךְ נֵ֝ר֗וֹ (באישון) [בֶּאֱשׁ֥וּן] חֹֽשֶׁךְ׃
verses.king_james: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: hæreditas ad quam festinatur in principio,  in novissimo benedictione carebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֭חֲלָה (מבחלת) [מְבֹהֶ֣לֶת] בָּרִאשׁוֹנָ֑ה וְ֝אַחֲרִיתָ֗הּ לֹ֣א תְבֹרָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: An inheritance may be gotten hastily at the beginning; but the end thereof shall not be blessed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dicas : Reddam malum :  exspecta Dominum, et liberabit te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תֹּאמַ֥ר אֲשַׁלְּמָה־רָ֑ע קַוֵּ֥ה לַ֝יהֹוָ֗ה וְיֹ֣שַֽׁע לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the Lord, and he shall save thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominatio est apud Dominum pondus et pondus ;  statera dolosa non est bona.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה אֶ֣בֶן וָאָ֑בֶן וּמֹאזְנֵ֖י מִרְמָ֣ה לֹא־טֽוֹב׃
verses.king_james: Divers weights are an abomination unto the Lord; and a false balance is not good.

verses.latin_vulgate: A Domino diriguntur gressus viri :  quis autem hominum intelligere potest viam suam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵיְהֹוָ֥ה מִצְעֲדֵי־גָ֑בֶר וְ֝אָדָ֗ם מַה־יָּבִ֥ין דַּרְכּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Man’s goings are of the Lord; how can a man then understand his own way?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ruina est homini devorare sanctos,  et post vota retractare.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מוֹקֵ֣שׁ אָ֭דָם יָ֣לַע קֹ֑דֶשׁ וְאַחַ֖ר נְדָרִ֣ים לְבַקֵּֽר׃
verses.king_james: It is a snare to the man who devoureth that which is holy, and after vows to make enquiry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissipat impios rex sapiens,  et incurvat super eos fornicem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְזָרֶ֣ה רְ֭שָׁעִים מֶ֣לֶךְ חָכָ֑ם וַיָּ֖שֶׁב עֲלֵיהֶ֣ם אוֹפָֽן׃
verses.king_james: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lucerna Domini spiraculum hominis,  quæ investigat omnia secreta ventris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֵ֣ר יְ֭הֹוָה נִשְׁמַ֣ת אָדָ֑ם חֹ֝פֵ֗שׂ כׇּל־חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן׃
verses.king_james: The spirit of man is the candle of the Lord, searching all the inward parts of the belly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misericordia et veritas custodiunt regem,  et roboratur clementia thronus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֶ֣סֶד וֶ֭אֱמֶת יִצְּרוּ־מֶ֑לֶךְ וְסָעַ֖ד בַּחֶ֣סֶד כִּסְאֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsultatio juvenum fortitudo eorum,  et dignitas senum canities.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּפְאֶ֣רֶת בַּחוּרִ֣ים כֹּחָ֑ם וַהֲדַ֖ר זְקֵנִ֣ים שֵׂיבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the gray head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Livor vulneris absterget mala,  et plagæ in secretioribus ventris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַבֻּר֣וֹת פֶּ֭צַע (תמריק) [תַּמְר֣וּק] בְּרָ֑ע וּ֝מַכּ֗וֹת חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן׃
verses.king_james: The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil: so do stripes the inward parts of the belly.
